blocks.content: Dean went to the back porch around eleven.

He didn't say he was going to make a call. He just went — quietly, with his phone, pulling the glass door closed behind him. Nadia was still at the stove. Cade was at the counter. Neither of them said anything.
He stood at the railing with the ocean in front of him and Marcus's mother's number on his screen and he looked at it for a long time before he pressed call.
She picked up on the second ring.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Dean.
Just his name. The way she said it told him she'd been waiting.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Mrs. Calloway. I — yes. It's me.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: The officer called last night. A man named Davenport. He was kind. He was very kind but he didn't — he couldn't tell me much.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I know. I'm sorry. I should have called last night and I couldn't — I didn't have the words yet.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: What happened to my son, Dean.
He closed his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We don't fully know yet. We found him — he was in his room. He'd gone up to rest before dinner and he didn't come down and we went to check on him and—

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Was he alone.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Yes. His door was locked. He was alone.
A silence on the line. Long. The ocean was doing its thing below the porch.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Was it his heart. The blood pressure — he told me he'd come off the medication but I always worried—

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We don't know yet. They're — they're still looking into it.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Still looking into it.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Yes.
He heard her breathe. A slow, careful breath. The kind you take when the alternative is falling apart.
